revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| ...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:御野県主神社鳥居.JPG|256px|玉祖神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] ==外部リンク== [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） [[Category:式内小社|みのあかたぬししんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|みのあかたぬししんしや]] [[Category:八尾市|みのあかたぬししんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:御野県主神社鳥居.JPG|256px|御野県主神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] ==外部リンク== [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） [[Category:式内小社|みのあかたぬししんしや]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|みのあかたぬししんしや]] [[Category:八尾市|みのあかたぬししんしや]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:御野県主神社鳥居.JPG|256px|御野県主神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に村社に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==式部之宮== 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]]万願寺村の集落はずれにあたり、万願寺村とも深い関わりがあったことを窺わせる。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] == 外部リンク == [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:八尾市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:御野県主神社鳥居.JPG|256px|御野県主神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==式部之宮== [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] == 外部リンク == [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:八尾市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: change Category
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:御野県主神社鳥居.JPG|256px|御野県主神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==式部之宮== [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] == 外部リンク == [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=御野縣主神社| 所在地=大阪府八尾市上之島町南1丁目71番地| 位置=| 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]]| 社格=式内小社、村社| 創建=| 例祭=| 画像=[[画像:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|256px|御野県主神社]]
御野縣主神社鳥居と拝殿 }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 ==歴史・概要== 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 ==式部之宮== [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 ==関連項目== *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/minoagata.htm 御野県主神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 2022年　テレビ東京　池の水全部抜く大作戦出演 7月2日　夏祭り 10月　第二週　土曜日　日曜日　秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 == 祭事 == * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == その他 == * [[2022年]]、[[テレビ東京]]「[[緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦]]」出演 == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]上之島町南1丁目71番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[村社]] | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=御野縣主
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 == 祭事 == * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == その他 == * [[2022年]]、[[テレビ東京]]「[[緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦]]」出演 == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|御野縣主神社|version=107257174|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]上之島町南1丁目71番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[村社]] | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=御野縣主
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 == 祭事 == * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == その他 == * [[2022年]]、[[テレビ東京]]「[[緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦]]」出演 == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] ==End Japanese==

Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe and Quiver]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|御野縣主神社|version=107257174|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]上之島町南1丁目71番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[角凝魂命]]、[[天湯川田奈命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）、旧[[村社]] | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=御野縣主
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|式内小社]]で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧[[河内国]][[河内郡 (大阪府)|河内郡]]上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、[[延喜式神名帳]]に河内国[[若江郡]]の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に[[村社]]に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた[[玉串川|玉櫛川]]（旧[[大和川]]）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 == 祭事 == * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == その他 == * [[2022年]]、[[テレビ東京]]「[[緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦]]」出演 == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧[[高安郡]][[東山本町|万願寺村]]の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *[[三野郷村]] *[[河内山本]] *[[東山本町]] {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は村社。 == 歴史・概要 == 旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|河内郡 (大阪府)|Q11553458|河内郡}}上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に河内国{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に{{jalink|村社}}に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた{{jalink|玉串川|Q11572360|玉櫛川}}（旧{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 == 祭事 == * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） == その他 == * {{jalink|2022年|Q49625}}、{{jalink|テレビ東京|Q734663}}「{{jalink|緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦|Q30938102}}」出演 == 式部之宮 == [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧{{jalink|高安郡}}{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717|万願寺村}}の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == *{{jalink|三野郷村|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|河内山本|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

{{nihongo|'''Mino Agatanushi Shrine'''|御野縣主神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}上之島町南1丁目71番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|角凝魂命}}、{{jalink|天湯川田奈命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=御野縣主
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は村社。 === 歴史・概要 === 旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|河内郡 (大阪府)|Q11553458|河内郡}}上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に河内国{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた{{jalink|玉串川|Q11572360|玉櫛川}}（旧{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 === 祭事 === * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） === その他 === * {{jalink|2022年|Q49625}}、{{jalink|テレビ東京|Q734663}}「{{jalink|緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦|Q30938102}}」出演 === 式部之宮 === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧{{jalink|高安郡}}{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717|万願寺村}}の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|三野郷村|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|河内山本|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=御野縣主神社 | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|御野県主神社]]
鳥居と拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}上之島町南1丁目71番地 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|角凝魂命}}、{{jalink|天湯川田奈命}} | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=御野縣主
神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は村社。 === 歴史・概要 === 旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|河内郡 (大阪府)|Q11553458|河内郡}}上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に河内国{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた{{jalink|玉串川|Q11572360|玉櫛川}}（旧{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 === 祭事 === * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） === その他 === * {{jalink|2022年|Q49625}}、{{jalink|テレビ東京|Q734663}}「{{jalink|緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦|Q30938102}}」出演 === 式部之宮 === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧{{jalink|高安郡}}{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717|万願寺村}}の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|三野郷村|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|河内山本|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''御野縣主神社'''（みのあがたぬしじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|八尾市|Q490872}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳#式内社の社格|Q11064932|式内小社}}で、旧社格は村社。 === 歴史・概要 === 旧{{jalink|河内国|Q1047150}}{{jalink|河内郡 (大阪府)|Q11553458|河内郡}}上之島村（八尾市上之島地区）の氏神。かつてこの地一帯を支配していた豪族・美努連（みぬのむらじ）、三野縣主一族の祖神である。 創建の年代は不詳であるが、{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に河内国{{jalink|若江郡|Q11616589}}の御野縣主神社二座と記載されていることから平安時代以前にはあったとされる。明治5年（1872年）に{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列せられた。 境内西側は小高い丘になっており、かつて西側に流れていた{{jalink|玉串川|Q11572360|玉櫛川}}（旧{{jalink|大和川|Q9001527}}）の堤防跡であり、現在ではここだけにしか残っていないとされる。八尾市の保全樹林に指定され、貴重な史跡となっている。境内の南に御野縣池がある。 === 祭事 === * 7月2日 - 夏祭り * 10月第2週土曜日・日曜日 - 秋祭り（布団太鼓巡行） === その他 === * {{jalink|2022年|Q49625}}、{{jalink|テレビ東京|Q734663}}「{{jalink|緊急SOS!池の水ぜんぶ抜く大作戦|Q30938102}}」出演 === 式部之宮 === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|式部御野縣主神社]] 本社のある場所から少し南、東山本町5丁目公園の脇にもう一座があり、「式部御野縣主神社」（式部之宮）とよばれる。こちらは狭い敷地内に社殿があるのみ。ここは旧{{jalink|高安郡}}{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717|万願寺村}}の集落はずれにあたり、かつて江戸時代中期に水利権争いで村が分裂し、式部集落が南の万願寺村に移ったときに分霊を勧請したものである。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} === 関連項目 === *{{jalink|三野郷村|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|河内山本|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|東山本町|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{jalink|Tamagushi River|Q11572360|Tamagushi River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{jalink|2022|Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{jalink|Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{jalink|Takayasu District}}{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Mino-gō Village|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{jalink|Tamagushi River|Q11572360|Tamagushi River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{jalink|2022|Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{jalink|Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{jalink|Takayasu District}}{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Mino-gō Village|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{jalink|Tamagushi River|Q11572360|Tamagushi River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{jalink|2022|Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{jalink|Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{jalink|Takayasu District}}{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Mino-gō Village|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{jalink|Tamagushi River|Q11572360|Tamagushi River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{jalink|2022|Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{jalink|Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{jalink|Takayasu District}}{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Mino-gō Village|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{jalink|Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto}}, {{jalink|Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto}} | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{jalink|Kawachi Province|Q1047150}}{{jalink|Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{jalink|Wakae District|Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{jalink|village shrine|Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{jalink|Tamagushi River|Q11572360|Tamagushi River}} (old {{jalink|Yamato River|Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{jalink|2022|Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{jalink|Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{jalink|Takayasu District}}{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{jalink|Mino-gō Village|Q98582346}} *{{jalink|Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399}} *{{jalink|Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11489340}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]]

This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みのあかたぬししんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:三野郷]][[Category:qq]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:三野郷]]

{{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|WD=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|WD=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|WD=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|WD=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|WD=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|WD=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|WD=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|WD=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|WD=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|WD=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:三野郷]]

== {{出典の明記|date=April 2020}} {{神社 | 名称=Mino Agatanushi Shrine | 画像=[[File:Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|250px|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}} Kaminoshima-chō Minami 1-71 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsunogori no Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-yukawata-na-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small), former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建= | 本殿= | 例祭= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Mino Agatanushi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Mino Agatanushi Shrine''' (みのあがたぬしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|qid=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha small shrine|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and its former shrine rank was village shrine. === History / Overview === It is the tutelary shrine of former Kaminoshima Village (Kaminoshima area of Yao City) in old {{ill|lt=Kawachi Province|Q1047150|qid=Q1047150|1=Kawachi Province}}{{ill|lt=Kawachi District (Osaka)|Q11553458|qid=Q11553458|lt=Kawachi District|1=Kawachi district}}. It enshrines the ancestral deity of powerful families that once ruled this area: the Mino no Muraji and Mino Agatanushi line. The date of foundation is unknown, but because the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} records two seats of Mino Agatanushi Shrine in {{ill|lt=Wakae District|Q11616589|qid=Q11616589|1=Wakae district}}, Kawachi Province, it is considered to have existed before the Heian period. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. The west side of the precinct is a small rise, the former embankment trace of {{ill|lt=Tamagushi River|Q11572360|qid=Q11572360|lt=Tamagushi River|1=Tamagushi River}} (old {{ill|lt=Yamato River|Q9001527|qid=Q9001527|1=Yamato River}}), once flowing on the west side. It is said to survive only here today. It is designated as protected woodland by Yao City and is a valuable historic site. South of the precinct is Mino Agata Pond. === Festivals === * July 2 - Summer festival * Second Saturday/Sunday of October - Autumn festival (futon-daiko procession) === Other === * {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}}, appeared on TV Tokyo’s {{ill|lt=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!|Q30938102|qid=Q30938102|1=Emergency SOS! We Drained the Pond!}} === Shikibe-no-Miya === [[ファイル:Shikibe-Minoagatanushi Jinja01.JPG|thumb|right|200px|Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine]] Slightly south of the main shrine, by Higashiyamamotochō 5-chōme Park, there is another seat called “Shikibe Mino Agatanushi Shrine” (Shikibe-no-Miya). It has only a shrine building on a small plot. This place was at the edge of a settlement in former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takayasu District|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|qid=Q11526717|lt=Manganji Village|1=Higashiyamamotochō}}; in the mid-Edo period, when the village split in a water-rights dispute and the Shikibe settlement moved south to Manganji Village, a divided spirit was invited there. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === *{{ill|lt=Mino-gō Village|Q98582346|qid=Q98582346|1=Minogō}} *{{ill|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto|Q11553399|qid=Q11553399|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto}} *{{ill|lt=Higashiyamamotochō|Q11526717|qid=Q11526717|1=Higashiyamamotochō}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Mino Agatanushi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:三野郷]]
